}\n196\t\n197\t    /// Get workers grouped by model.\n198\t    pub fn workers_by_model(&self) -> HashMap<String, Vec<&WorkerStatusFile>> {\n199\t        let mut by_model: HashMap<String, }\n221\t\n222\t        // Check both status files and tmux sessions\n223\t        if !self.has_any_workers() {\n224\t            return \"No workers found.\\n\\n\\\n225\t                    Start format_activity_log(&self) -> String {\n432\t        if !self.is_loaded() {\n433\t            return \"Loading activity data...\".to_string();\n434\t        }\n435\t\n436\t        if !self.has_any_workers() {\n437\t            return \"No recent activity.\\n\\n\\\n438\t                    Activity will appear here as workers\\n\\\n439\t                    complete tasks and update their status.\"\n440\t                .to_string();\n441\t        }\n442\t\n443\t        let mut lines = Vec::new();\n444\t\n445\t        // Use status file workers if available (more detailed)\n446\t             .cmp(&a.last_activity)\n451\t                    .then(b.started_at.cmp(&a.started_at))\n452\t            });\n453\t\n454\t            let now = chrono::Local::now();\n455\t\n456\t activity.format(\"%H:%M:%S\").to_string()\n459\t                } else if let Some(started) = worker.started_at {\n460\t                    started.format(\"%H:%M:%S\").to_string()\n461\t };\n474\t\n475\t                let message = match worker.status {\n476\t                    WorkerStatus::Active => {\n477\t                        if let Some(task) = &worker.current_task {\n478\t WorkerStatus::Stopped => format!(\"{} stopped\", worker.worker_id),\n487\t                    WorkerStatus::Error => format!(\"{} error\", worker.worker_id),\n488\t                    WorkerStatus::Paused => format!(\"{} paused\", worker.worker_id),\n489\t                };\n490\t\n491\t                lines.push(format!(\"{} {} {}\", time_str, icon, message));\n492\t tmux.workers.iter().collect();\n496\t            workers.sort_by_key(|b| std::cmp::Reverse(b.last_activity));\n497\t\n498\t            for worker in workers.iter().take(10) {\n499\t                let time_str = worker.last_activity.format(\"%H:%M:%S\").to_string();\n500\t                let icon = if worker.is_attached { \"⟳\" } else { \"💤\" };\n501\t                let status = if worker.is_attached {\n502\t                    \"attached\"\n503\t                } else {\n504\t                    \"detached\"\n505\t                };\n506\t                let message = format!(\"{} {} ({})\", worker.session_name, status, worker.age());\n507\t\n508\t                lines.push(format!(\"{} {} {}\", time_str, icon, message));\n509\t            }\n510\t        }\n511\t\n512\t        if lines.is_empty() {\n513\t            lines.push(\"No recent activity.\".to_string());\n514\t        }\n515\t\n516\t        lines.join(\"\\n\")\n517\t    }\n518\t}\n519\t\n520\t/// Format a model identifier for display.\n521\tfn format_model_name(model: &str) -> String {\n522\t    // Common model name mappings\n523\t    let lower = model.to_lowercase();\n524\t    if lower.contains(\"glm-47\") || lower.contains(\"glm47\") || lower.contains(\"glm-4.7\") {\n525\t        \"GLM-4.7\".to_string()\n526\t    } else if lower.contains(\"sonnet\") {\n527\t        \"Sonnet\".to_string()\n528\t    } else if lower.contains(\"opus\") {\n529\t        \"Opus\".to_string()\n530\t    } else if lower.contains(\"haiku\") {\n531\t        \"Haiku\".to_string()\n532\t    } else if model.is_empty() {\n533\t        \"Unknown\".to_string()\n534\t    } else {\n535\t        model.to_string()\n536\t    }\n537\t}\n538\t\n539\t/// Format model name for table (shorter version).\n540\tfn format_model_name_short(model: &str) -> String {\n541\t    let name = format_model_name(model);\n542\t    truncate_string(&name, 8)\n543\t}\n544\t\n545\t/// Format worker status for display.\n546\tfn format_status(status: &WorkerStatus) -> String {\n547\t    match status {\n548\t        WorkerStatus::Active => \"Active\".to_string(),\n549\t        WorkerStatus::Idle => \"Idle\".to_string(),\n550\t        WorkerStatus::Starting => \"Starting\".to_string(),\n551\t        WorkerStatus::Failed => \"Failed\".to_string(),\n552\t        WorkerStatus::Stopped => \"Stopped\".to_string(),\n553\t        WorkerStatus::Error => \"Error\".to_string(),\n554\t        WorkerStatus::Paused => \"Paused\".to_string(),\n555\t    }\n556\t}\n557\t\n558\t/// Truncate a string to a maximum length.\n559\tfn truncate_string(s: &str, max_len: usize) -> String {\n560\t    if s.len() <= max_len {\n561\t        s.to_string()\n562\t    } else if max_len > 2 {\n563\t        format!(\"{}…\", &s[..max_len - 1])\n564\t    } else {\n565\t        s[..max_len].to_string()\n566\t    }\n567\t}\n568\t\n569\t/// Interval for tmux discovery polling (5 seconds).\n570\tconst TMUX_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL_SECS: u64 = 5;\n571\t\n572\t/// Interval for cost data polling (10 seconds).\n573\tconst (500ms).\n579\tconst LOG_WATCHER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS: u64 = 500;\n580\t\n581\t/// Interval for subscription data polling (60 seconds).\n582\tconst SUBSCRIPTION_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS: u64 = 60;\n583\t\n584\t/// Interval for health monitoring (30 seconds).\n585\tconst HEALTH_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS: u64 = 30;\n586\t\n587\t/// Interval for activity monitoring (15 seconds).\n588\tconst ACTIVITY_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS: u64 = 15;\n589\t\n590\t/// Data manager that handles the StatusWatcher, BeadManager, CostDatabase, LogWatcher, HealthMonitor, AlertManager, ActivityMonitor, and provides formatted data.\n591\tpub struct DataManager {\n592\t    /// StatusWatcher for real-time updates\n593\t    watcher: Option<StatusWatcher>,\n594\t    /// Cached worker data\n595\t    pub worker_data: }\n674\t\n675\t    /// Take and clear the dirty flag.\n676\t    pub fn take_dirty(&mut self) -> bool {\n677\t        if self.dirty {\n678\t            self.dirty = false;\n679\t            true\n680\t        } else {\n681\t            false\n682\t        }\n683\t    }\n684\t\n685\t    /// Create a new DataManager, initializing the StatusWatcher, BeadManager, and CostDatabase.\n686\t    pub fn new() CostDatabase::open(&db_path).ok()\n844\t        }\n845\t    }\n846\t\n847\t    /// Create a DataManager with a custom status directory.\n848\t    pub fn with_status_dir(status_dir: std::path::PathBuf)   }\n945\t\n946\t    /// Build SubscriptionData from the tracker.\n947\t    fn build_subscription_data(tracker: &SubscriptionTracker) -> SubscriptionData {\n948\t        use crate::subscription_panel::{\n949\t            ResetPeriod, SubscriptionAction, SubscriptionService, SubscriptionStatus,\n950\t        };\n951\t        use chrono::{Duration, Utc};\n952\t        use tracing::info;\n953\t\n954\t        info!(\n955\t            \"Building subscription data, tracker has {} subscriptions\",\n956\t            tracker.len()\n957\t        );\n958\t\n959\t        let mut data = SubscriptionData::new();\n960\t\n961\t        for summary in tracker.get_summaries() {\n962\t            info!(\n963\t                \"Processing subscription: {} (usage: {}/{:?})\",\n964\t                summary.name, summary.quota_used, summary.quota_limit\n965\t            );\n966\t            // Map subscription name to service type\n967\t            let service = if summary.name.to_lowercase().contains(\"anthropic\")\n968\t                || summary.name.to_lowercase().contains(\"claude\")\n969\t            {\n970\t                SubscriptionService::ClaudePro\n971\t            } else if summary.name.to_lowercase().contains(\"openai\")\n972\t                || summary.name.to_lowercase().contains(\"chatgpt\")\n973\t            {\n974\t                SubscriptionService::ChatGPTPlus\n975\t            } else if summary.name.to_lowercase().contains(\"cursor\") {\n976\t                SubscriptionService::CursorPro\n977\t            } else {\n978\t                SubscriptionService::DeepSeekAPI // Default fallback\n979\t            };\n980\t\n981\t            let mut status = SubscriptionStatus::new(service);\n982\t\n983\t            // Set usage if we have a limit\n984\t            if let Some(limit) = summary.quota_limit {\n985\t                status = status.with_usage(summary.quota_used as u64, limit as u64, \"tokens\");\n986\t            }\n987\t\n988\t            // Set billing period\n989\t            let days_remaining = tracker.days_until_renewal(&summary.name).unwrap_or(30);\n990\t            let reset_period = if days_remaining <= 1 {\n991\t                ResetPeriod::Daily\n992\t            } else if days_remaining <= 7 {\n993\t                ResetPeriod::Weekly\n994\t            } else {\n995\t                ResetPeriod::Monthly\n996\t            };\n997\t            status = status.with_reset(Utc::now() + Duration::days(days_remaining), reset_period);\n998\t\n999\t            // Set active status\n1000\t            status = status.with_active(true);\n1001\t\n1002\t            // Map quota status to action\n1003\t            let _action = match summary.status {\n1004\t                forge_cost::QuotaStatus::OnPace => SubscriptionAction::OnPace,\n1005\t                forge_cost::QuotaStatus::Accelerate => SubscriptionAction::Accelerate,\n1006\t                forge_cost::QuotaStatus::MaxOut => SubscriptionAction::MaxOut,\n1007\t                forge_cost::QuotaStatus::Depleted => SubscriptionAction::OverQuota,\n1008\t            };\n1009\t\n1010\t            // Check for alerts\n1011\t            let alert = tracker.get_alert(&summary.name);\n1012\t            if alert.is_alert() {\n1013\t                // Mark as critical/high usage in the status\n1014\t                if matches!(\n1015\t                    alert,\n1016\t                    forge_cost::SubscriptionAlert::Critical\n1017\t                        | forge_cost::SubscriptionAlert::Depleted\n1018\t                ) {\n1019\t                    // Override action to show urgency\n1020\t                    status.current_usage = status.limit.unwrap_or(100) - 1; // Show 99% used\n1021\t                }\n1022\t            }\n1023\t\n1024\t            data.subscriptions.push(status);\n1025\t        }\n1026\t\n1027\t        data.last_updated = Some(Utc::now());\n1028\t        data\n1029\t    }\n1030\t\n1031\t    /// Poll for updates from the StatusWatcher, BeadManager, CostDatabase, and tmux discovery.\n1032\t    ///\n1033\t    /// This should be called regularly (e.g., in the event loop).\n1034\t    /// Returns true if any data changed.\n1035\t    pub fn poll_updates(&mut self) -> bool {\n1036\t        let mut changed = false;\n1037\t        let previous_worker_count = self.cached_worker_count;\n1038\t        let previous_bead_count = self.cached_bead_count;\n1039\t\n1040\t        // Poll status watcher\n1041\t        if let Some(ref mut watcher) = self.watcher {\n1042\t            // Drain all available events and check if any were received\n1043\t            let mut events_received = false;\n1044\t            while watcher.try_recv().is_some() {\n1045\t                events_received = true;\n1046\t            }\n1047\t\n1048\t            // Only update if events were received\n1049\t            if events_received {\n1050\t                // Clone current workers to detect changes (avoids borrow issues)\n1051\t                let current_workers = watcher.workers().clone();\n1052\t\n1053\t                // Detect changes before updating\n1054\t                for (worker_id, worker) in &current_workers {\n1055\t                    let prev_status = self.prev_worker_statuses.get(worker_id);\n1056\t\n1057\t                    match prev_status {\n1058\t                        None => {\n1059\t                            // New worker discovered\n1060\t                            self.activity_data.push(\n1061\t                                ActivityEntry::new(\n1062\t                                    ActivityEventType::WorkerSpawn,\n1063\t                                    \"Worker spawned\",\n1064\t                                )\n1065\t                                .with_source(worker_id),\n1066\t                            );\n1067\t                        }\n1068\t                        Some(prev) if *prev != worker.status => {\n1069\t                            // Status transition\n1070\t                            let message =\n1071\t                                format!(\"Status changed: {:?} → {:?}\", prev, worker.status);\n1072\t WorkerStatus::Failed | WorkerStatus::Error => {\n1075\t                                    ActivityEventType::Error\n1076\t                                }\n1077\t                                _ => ActivityEventType::WorkerTransition,\n1078\t                            };\n1079\t                            self.activity_data.push(\n1080\t                                ActivityEntry::new(event_type, message).with_source(worker_id),\n1081\t                            );\n1082\t\n1083\t                            // Add task info if available\n1084\t                            if let Some(ref task) = worker.current_task {\n1085\t                                self.activity_data.push(\n1086\t                                    ActivityEntry::new(\n1087\t                                        ActivityEventType::TaskPickup,\n1088\t                                        format!(\"Working on {}\", task),\n1089\t                                    )\n1090\t                                    .with_source(worker_id),\n1091\t                                );\n1092\t                            }\n1093\t                        }\n1094\t                        _ => {}\n1095\t                    }\n1096\t                }\n1097\t\n1098\t                // Check for workers that were removed\n1099\t                for worker_id in self.prev_worker_statuses.keys() ActivityEntry::new(ActivityEventType::WorkerStop, \"Worker stopped\")\n1103\t                                .with_source(worker_id),\n1104\t                        );\n1105\t                    }\n1106\t                }\n1107\t\n1108\t                // Update previous statuses\n1109\t                self.prev_worker_statuses = current_workers\n1110\t                    .iter()\n1111\t                    .map(|(k, v)| (k.clone(), v.status))\n1112\t                    .collect();\n1113\t\n1114\t                // Now update worker data\n1115\t                self.worker_data.update_from_watcher(watcher);\n1116\t                changed = true;\n1117\t            }\n1118\t        }\n1119\t\n1120\t        // Poll bead manager for task queue updates\n1121\t        let beads_changed = self.bead_manager.poll_updates();\n1122\t        if beads_changed {\n1123\t            // Add activity entries for bead changes\n1124\t            self.detect_bead_changes();\n1125\t            changed = true;\n1126\t        }\n1127\t\n1128\t        // Periodically poll tmux discovery (less frequently)\n1129\t        let should_poll_tmux = {\n1144\t            self.poll_cost_data();\n1145\t            self.last_cost_poll = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n1146\t        }\n1147\t\n1148\t        // Periodically poll metrics data (every {\n1174\t            self.poll_subscription_data();\n1175\t            self.last_subscription_poll = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n1176\t        }\n1177\t\n1178\t        // Periodically poll .last_activity_poll\n1191\t            .map_or(true, |t| t.elapsed().as_secs() >= ACTIVITY_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS);\n1192\t\n1193\t        if should_poll_activity {\n1194\t self.cached_bead_count\n1215\t        {\n1216\t            self.dirty = true;\n1217\t        }\n1218\t\n1219\t        self.dirty\n1220\t    }\n1221\t\n1222\t    /// Detect bead/task changes and add activity entries.\n1223\t    fn detect_bead_changes(&mut self) {\n1224\t        // Add a generic activity entry when bead data changes\n1225\t        // In a production system, we'd track specific bead state changes\n1226\t        let total_beads = self.bead_manager.total_bead_count();\n1227\t        if total_beads > 0 {\n1228\t            // This is a simple notification - could be enhanced to pub fn add_activity(\n1235\t        &mut self,\n1236\t        event_type: ActivityEventType,\n1237\t        source: Option<&str>,\n1238\t        message: impl Into<String>,\n1239\t    ) {\n1240\t }\n1263\t\n1264\t    /// Record a key event.\n1265\t    pub fn record_event(&mut self) {\n1266\t        self.perf_metrics.record_event();\n1267\t    }\n1268\t\n1269\t    /// Record a worker }\n1304\t\n1305\t    /// Get mutable access to activity log data (for scroll control).\n1306\t    pub fn activity_log_mut(&mut self) -> &mut ActivityLogData {\n1307\t        &mut self.activity_data\n1308\t    }\n1309\t\n1310\t    /// Poll health monitor for worker health status.\n1311\t    fn poll_health_monitor(&mut self) {\n1312\t        let Some(ref mut monitor) = self.health_monitor else {\n1313\t            return;\n1314\t        };\n1315\t\n1316\t        match monitor.check_all_health() {\n1317\t            Ok(health_status) => {\n1318\t                // .get(worker_id)\n1325\t                        .map(|h| h.is_healthy)\n1326\t                        .unwrap_or(true);\n1327\t\n1328\t                    // Log status transitions\n1329\t                    if !health.is_healthy && prev_was_healthy {\n1330\t                        // Worker just became unhealthy\n1331\t                        let level = health.health_level();\n1332\t                        let msg = match level {\n1333\t                            forge_worker::health::HealthLevel::Degraded => {\n1334\t                                format!(\n1335\t                                    \"Worker degraded: {} (score: {:.0}%)\",\n1336\t                                    health.primary_error.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"unknown\"),\n1337\t                                    health.health_score * 100.0\n1338\t                                )\n1339\t                            }\n1340\t                            forge_worker::health::HealthLevel::Unhealthy => {\n1341\t health.primary_error.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"unknown\"),\n1344\t                                    health.health_score * 100.0\n1345\t                                )\n1346\t .contains(&HealthCheckType::ResponseHealth)\n1377\t                            {\n1378\t                                (AlertType::WorkerUnresponsive, health.primary_error.clone())\n1379\t                     .with_source(worker_id),\n1400\t                                );\n1401\t                            }\n1402\t                        }\n1403\t                    }\n1404\t\n1405\t                    // Check for auto-restart trigger\n1406\t                    if health.should_auto_restart {\n1407\t                        let msg = format!(\n1408\t }\n1456\t\n1457\t                let unhealthy_count = health_status.values().filter(|h| !h.is_healthy).count();\n1458\t                let degraded_count = health_status\n1459\t                    .values()\n1460\t                    .filter(|h| {\n1461\t                        matches!(\n1462\t                            h.health_level(),\n1463\t .unwrap_or(false);\n1516\t\n1517\t            if activity.state == ActivityState::Stuck && !was_stuck {\n1518\t                // Worker just became stuck - raise alert and log activity\n1519\t self.activity_data.push(\n1524\t                    ActivityEntry::new(ActivityEventType::Warning, msg.clone())\n1525\t                        .with_source(worker_id),\n1526\t                );\n1527\t\n1528\t                // Raise alert for stuck worker\n1529\t                self.alert_manager.raise(\n1530\t                    AlertType::TaskStuck,\n1531\t for updates.\n1571\t    fn poll_subscription_data(&mut self) {\n1572\t        use chrono::{Duration, Utc};\n1573\t        use tracing::info;\n1574\t\n1575\t        let Some(ref db) = self.cost_db else {\n1576\t            // No database, just rebuild from tracker\n1577\t            self.subscription_data = Self::build_subscription_data(&self.subscription_tracker);\n1578\t            return;\n1579\t        };\n1580\t\n1581\t        // 1. Check and auto-reset billing periods that have ended\n1582\t        if let Ok(reset_count) = self.subscription_tracker.check_and_reset_billing(db) {\n1583\t            if reset_count > 0 {\n1584\t                info!(\"Reset {} subscription billing periods\", reset_count);\n1585\t                self.dirty = true;\n1586\t            }\n1587\t        }\n1588\t\n1589\t        // 2. Track recent API calls against subscriptions\n1590\t        // Get API calls from the last polling interval (5 minutes)\n1591\t        let since = Utc::now() - Duration::seconds(300); // 5 minutes\n1592\t        if let Ok(recent_calls) = db.get_api_calls_since(since) {\n1593\t            for call in recent_calls {\n1594\t                // Find which subscription this model belongs to\n1595\t                if let Some(ref sub_name) = self.subscription_tracker\n1596\t                    .find_subscription_for_model(&call.model)\n1597\t                {\n1598\t                    // Track total tokens used\n1599\t                    let total_tokens = call.total_tokens();\n1600\t\n1601\t                    // Record usage and update subscription quota\n1602\t                    if let Err(e) = db.increment_subscription_usage(sub_name, total_tokens) {\n1603\t                        tracing::warn!(\n1604\t                            \"Failed to increment subscription usage for {}: {}\",\n1605\t                            sub_name, e\n1606\t                        );\n1607\t                    } else {\n1608\t                        // Update local tracker cache\n1609\t                        self.subscription_tracker.increment_usage(sub_name, total_tokens);\n1610\t\n1611\t                        // Also record detailed usage event\n1612\t                        use .with_api_call(call.id.unwrap_or(0));\n1618\t                            let _ = db.record_subscription_usage(&record);\n1619\t                        }\n1620\t                    }\n1621\t                }\n1622\t            }\n1623\t        }\n1624\t\n1625\t        // 3. Reload subscriptions from database to get updated quota_used values\n1626\t        if let Err(e) = self.subscription_tracker.load_from_database(db) {\n1627\t            tracing::warn!(\"Failed to reload subscriptions from database: {}\", e);\n1628\t        }\n1629\t\n1630\t        // 4. Rebuild subscription data for display\n1631\t        self.subscription_data = Self::build_subscription_data(&self.subscription_tracker);\n1632\t\n1633\t        // 5. Check for critical alerts\n1634\t        if self.subscription_tracker.has_critical_alert() {\n1635\t            self.dirty = true;\n1636\t        }\n1637\t    }\n1638\t\n1639\t    /// Poll cost database for updates.\n1640\t    fn poll_cost_data(&mut self) {\n1641\t        let Some(ref db) = self.cost_db else {\n1642\t            self.cost_data = CostPanelData::with_error(\"Cost database not initialized\");\n1643\t            return;\n1644\t        };\n1645\t\n1646\t        let query = CostQuery::new(db);\n1647\t\n1648\t        // Get today's costs\n1649\t        match query.get_today_costs() {\n1650\t            Ok(today) => {\n1651\t                self.cost_data.set_today(today);\n1652\t            }\n1653\t            Err(e) => {\n1654\t                self.cost_data.error = Some(format!(\"Failed to get today's costs: {}\", e));\n1655\t                return;\n1656\t            }\n1657\t        }\n1658\t\n1659\t        // Get this week's costs (last 7 days)\n1660\t        match query.get_weekly_costs() {\n1661\t            Ok(week) => {\n1662\t                self.cost_data.set_week(week);\n1663\t            }\n1664\t            Err(_) => {\n1665\t                // Weekly cost failure is non-critical\n1666\t            }\n1667\t        }\n1668\t\n1669\t        // Get current month's costs\n1670\t        match query.get_current_month_costs() {\n1671\t            Ok(monthly) => {\n1672\t                // Convert to model costs for display\n1673\t                let model_costs: Vec<forge_cost::ModelCost> = monthly\n1674\t                    .by_model\n1675\t                    .iter()\n1676\t                    .map(|b| forge_cost::ModelCost {\n1677\t                        model: b.model.clone(),\n1678\t                        total_cost_usd: b.total_cost_usd,\n1679\t                        call_count: b.call_count,\n1680\t                        avg_cost_per_call: if b.call_count > 0 {\n1681\t                            b.total_cost_usd / b.call_count as f64\n1682\t                        } else {\n1683\t                            0.0\n1684\t                        },\n1685\t                        total_tokens: b.input_tokens\n1686\t                            + b.output_tokens\n1687\t                            + b.cache_creation_tokens\n1688\t                            + b.cache_read_tokens,\n1689\t                    })\n1690\t                    .collect();\n1691\t\n1692\t                self.cost_data\n1693\t                    .set_monthly(monthly.total_cost_usd, model_costs);\n1694\t\n1695\t                // Build daily trend from monthly data\n1696\t                let trend: Vec<(chrono::NaiveDate, f64)> = monthly\n1697\t                    .by_day\n1698\t                    .iter()\n1699\t                    .map(|d| (d.date, d.total_cost_usd))\n1700\t                    .collect();\n1701\t                self.cost_data.set_daily_trend(trend);\n1702\t            }\n1703\t            Err(e) => {\n1704\t                self.cost_data.error = Some(format!(\"Failed to get monthly costs: {}\", e));\n1705\t            }\n1706\t        }\n1707\t\n1708\t        // Get projected costs\n1709\t        match query.get_projected_costs(None) {\n1710\t            Ok(projected) => {\n1711\t                self.cost_data.set_projected(projected);\n1712\t            }\n1713\t            Err(_) => {\n1714\t                // Projection failure is non-critical\n1715\t            }\n1716\t        }\n1717\t\n1718\t        // Get optimization data using CostOptimizer\n1719\t        let optimizer = forge_cost::CostOptimizer::new(db, forge_cost::OptimizerConfig::default());\n1720\t        match optimizer.generate_report() {\n1721\t            Ok(report) => {\n1722\t                self.cost_data.set_recommendations(report.recommendations);\n1723\t                self.cost_data.set_savings_achieved(report.savings_achieved);\n1724\t                self.cost_data\n1725\t                    .set_subscription_utilization(report.subscription_utilization);\n1726\t            }\n1727\t            Err(_) => {\n1728\t                // Optimization failure is non-critical\n1729\t            }\n1730\t        }\n1731\t\n1732\t        // Set default budget config (can be customized later)\n1733\t        self.cost_data.set_budget(BudgetConfig::default());\n1734\t\n1735\t        // Get worker cost breakdown (top 10 workers)\n1736\t        match query.get_today_worker_costs(10) {\n1737\t            Ok(mut workers) => {\n1738\t                // Calculate session total and mark expensive workers\n1739\t                let session_total: f64 = workers.iter().map(|w| w.total_cost_usd).sum();\n1740\t\n1741\t                // Set expensive threshold to 10% of session total or $1, whichever is higher\n1742\t                let threshold = (session_total * 0.1).max(1.0);\n1743\t                for worker in &mut workers {\n1744\t                    worker.is_expensive = worker.total_cost_usd >= threshold;\n1745\t                }\n1746\t\n1747\t                self.cost_data.set_worker_costs(workers);\n1748\t                self.cost_data.set_session_total(session_total);\n1749\t                self.cost_data.set_expensive_threshold(threshold);\n1750\t            }\n1751\t            Err(_) => {\n1752\t                // Worker cost failure is non-critical\n1753\t            }\n1754\t        }\n1755\t    }\n1756\t\n1757\t    /// Poll tmux for worker sessions.\n1758\t    fn poll_tmux_discovery(&mut self) {\n1759\t        if let Some(ref runtime) = self.runtime {\n1760\t            // Run tmux discovery asynchronously but block briefly for result\n1761\t            match runtime.block_on(async {\n1762\t                tokio::time::timeout(\n1763\t                    std::time::Duration::from_millis(500),\n1764\t                    forge_worker::discover_workers(),\n1765\t                )\n1766\t                .await\n1767\t            }) {\n1768\t                Ok(Ok(result)) => {\n1769\t                    self.worker_data.update_from_tmux(result);\n1770\t                }\n1771\t                Ok(Err(_)) | Err(_) => {\n1772\t                    // Discovery failed or timed out - that's okay, status files are primary\n1773\t                }\n1774\t            }\n1775\t        }\n1776\t    }\n1777\t\n1778\t    /// Poll metrics database for   // No stats for today yet - create empty stats\n1793\t                let empty_daily = forge_cost::DailyStat::new(today);\n1794\t                self.metrics_data.set_today(empty_daily);\n1795\t self.metrics_data.set_task_trend_7day(trend);\n1836\t            }\n1837\t            Err(_) => {\n1838\t                // Trend data failure is non-critical\n1839\t            }\n1840\t        }\n1841\t\n1842\t        // Get 7-day cost trend\n1843\t        if let Ok(trend) = db.get_7day_cost_trend() {\n1844\t            self.metrics_data.set_cost_trend_7day(trend);\n1845\t self.metrics_data.set_model_performance_7day(models);\n1855\t        }\n1856\t\n1857\t        // Get 7-day worker efficiency aggregation\n1858\t        if let Ok(workers) = API usage updates.\n1869\t    fn poll_log_watcher(&mut self) {\n1870\t        // Collect calls to persist to database\n1871\t        let mut calls_to_persist: Vec<forge_cost::ApiCall> = Vec::new();\n1872\t\n1873\t        // Process events from log watcher channel\n1874\t        if let Some(ref rx) = self.log_rx {\n1875\t            while let Ok(event) = rx.try_recv() {\n1876\t                match event {\n1877\t                    LogWatcherEvent::ApiCallParsed { call } => {\n1878\t                        // Update real-time metrics\n1879\t                        self.realtime_metrics.record_call(&call);\n1880\t\n1881\t                        // Collect for database persistence\n1882\t                        calls_to_persist.push(call.clone());\n1883\t\n1884\t                        // Add activity entry for API call\n1885\t                        let cost_str = if call.cost_usd >= 1.0 {\n1886\t                            format!(\"${:.2}\", call.cost_usd)\n1887\t                        } else if call.cost_usd >= 0.01 {\n1888\t                            format!(\"${:.3}\", call.cost_usd)\n1889\t                        } else {\n1890\t                            format!(\"${:.4}\", call.cost_usd)\n1891\t                        };\n1892\t                        let msg = format!(\n1893\t                            \"{} API call - {} in, {} out ({})\",\n1894\t                            call.model, call.input_tokens, call.output_tokens, cost_str\n1895\t                        );\n1896\t                        self.activity_data.push(\n1897\t                            ActivityEntry::new(ActivityEventType::ApiCall, msg)\n1898\t                                .with_source(&call.worker_id),\n1899\t                        );\n1900\t\n1901\t                        // The real-time metrics are now available for panels\n1902\t                        // via self.realtime_metrics\n1903\t                        self.dirty = true;\n1904\t                    }\n1905\t                    LogWatcherEvent::FileDiscovered { path, worker_id } => {\n1906\t                        tracing::debug!(\"Log file discovered: {:?} for worker {}\", path, worker_id);\n1907\t                    }\n1908\t                    LogWatcherEvent::FileRotated { path, worker_id } => {\n1909\t                        tracing::debug!(\"Log file rotated: {:?} for worker {}\", path, worker_id);\n1910\t                    }\n1911\t                    LogWatcherEvent::Error { message } => {\n1912\t                        tracing::warn!(\"Log watcher error: {}\", message);\n1913\t                    }\n1914\t                }\n1915\t            }\n1916\t        }\n1917\t\n1918\t        // Also poll the log watcher directly for any new content\n1919\t        if let Some(ref mut watcher) = self.log_watcher {\n1920\t            let events = watcher.poll();\n1921\t            for event in events {\n1922\t                if let LogWatcherEvent::ApiCallParsed { call } = event {\n1923\t                    self.realtime_metrics.record_call(&call);\n1924\t                    calls_to_persist.push(call);\n1925\t                    self.dirty = true;\n1926\t                }\n1927\t            }\n1928\t        }\n1929\t\n1930\t        // Persist collected API calls to the database for historical tracking\n1931\t        if !calls_to_persist.is_empty() {\n1932\t            if let Some(ref db) = self.cost_db {\n1933\t                if let Err(e) = db.insert_api_calls(&calls_to_persist) {\n1934\t                    tracing::warn!(\n1935\t                        \"Failed to persist {} API calls to database: {}\",\n1936\t                        calls_to_persist.len(),\n1937\t                        e\n1938\t                    );\n1939\t                } else {\n1940\t                    tracing::debug!(\"Persisted {} API calls to database\", calls_to_persist.len());\n1941\t                }\n1942\t            }\n1943\t        }\n1944\t\n1945\t        // Propagate realtime metrics to cost_data and metrics_data for panel display\n1946\t        if self.realtime_metrics.has_data() {\n1947\t            self.cost_data.set_realtime(self.realtime_metrics.clone());\n1948\t            self.metrics_data\n1949\t                .set_realtime(self.realtime_metrics.clone());\n1950\t        }\n1951\t    }\n1952\t\n1953\t    /// Check if there was an initialization error.\n1954\t    pub fn init_error(&self) -> Option<&str> {\n1955\t        self.init_error.as_deref()\n1956\t    }\n1957\t\n1958\t    /// }\n2013\t\n2014\t    /// Check if visual flash is currently active.\n2015\t    pub fn is_flashing(&self) -> bool {\n2016\t        self.alert_notifier.is_flashing()\n2017\t    }\n2018\t\n2019\t    // ========== Model Routing & Complexity Scoring ==========\n2020\t\n2021\t    /// Score a task's complexity (0-100).\n2022\t    ///\n2023\t    /// Uses the ComplexityScorer to analyze task title, description, labels,\n2024\t    /// and other factors to determine the appropriate model tier.\n2025\t    pub fn score_task_complexity(\n2026\t        &self,\n2027\t        title: &str,\n2028\t        description: Option<&str>,\n2029\t        labels: &[String],\n2030\t    ) -> u32 {\n2031\t        let mut context = TaskContext::new(title)\n2032\t            .with_labels(labels.to_vec());\n2033\t\n2034\t        if let Some(desc) = description {\n2035\t            context = context.with_description(desc);\n2036\t        }\n2037\t\n2038\t        self.complexity_scorer.score(&context).score\n2039\t    }\n2040\t\n2041\t    /// Route a task to the appropriate model tier.\n2042\t    ///\n2043\t    /// Uses the Router to make intelligent routing decisions based on\n2044\t    /// task priority, complexity, and subscription availability.\n2045\t    pub fn route_task(\n2046\t        &mut self,\n2047\t        bead_id: &str,\n2048\t        title: &str,\n2049\t        priority: Priority,\n2050\t        labels: Vec<String>,\n2051\t    ) -> Option<forge_worker::router::RoutingDecision> {\n2052\t        // Score complexity\n2053\t        let complexity_score = self.score_task_complexity(title, None, &labels);\n2054\t\n2055\t        // Create task metadata\n2056\t        let task = TaskMetadata::new(bead_id, priority)\n2057\t            .with_complexity(complexity_score)\n2058\t            .with_labels(labels);\n2059\t\n2060\t        // Make routing decision\n2061\t        match self.router.route(&task) {\n2062\t            Ok(decision) => {\n2063\t                // Update routing data for display\n2064\t                self.routing_data.total_decisions += 1;\n2065\t                *self.routing_data.by_tier.entry(decision.tier).or_insert(0) += 1;\n2066\t                *self.routing_data.by_model.entry(decision.model_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n2067\t                *self.routing_data.by_reason.entry(decision.reason).or_insert(0) += 1;\n2068\t\n2069\t                // Track recent decisions (keep last 20)\n2070\t                self.routing_data.recent_decisions.push(decision.clone());\n2071\t                if self.routing_data.recent_decisions.len() > 20 {\n2072\t                    self.routing_data.recent_decisions.remove(0);\n2073\t                }\n2074\t\n2075\t                // Update complexity distribution\n2076\t                match decision.tier {\n2077\t                    forge_core::types::WorkerTier::Budget => {\n2078\t                        self.routing_data.complexity_distribution.0 += 1;\n2079\t                    }\n2080\t                    forge_core::types::WorkerTier::Standard => {\n2081\t                        self.routing_data.complexity_distribution.1 += 1;\n2082\t                    }\n2083\t                    forge_core::types::WorkerTier::Premium => {\n2084\t                        self.routing_data.complexity_distribution.2 += 1;\n2085\t                    }\n2086\t                }\n2087\t\n2088\t                // Calculate average complexity\n2089\t                let total = self.routing_data.total_decisions;\n2090\t                self.routing_data.avg_complexity =\n2091\t                    (self.routing_data.avg_complexity * (total - 1) as f64 + complexity_score as f64)\n2092\t                    / total as f64;\n2093\t\n2094\t                // Calculate savings\n2095\t                self.routing_data.calculate_savings();\n2096\t                self.routing_data.last_update = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n2097\t                self.routing_data.is_loading = false;\n2098\t\n2099\t                Some(decision)\n2100\t            }\n2101\t            Err(e) => {\n2102\t                tracing::warn!(\"Routing decision failed for {}: {}\", bead_id, e);\n2103\t                None\n2104\t            }\n2105\t        }\n2106\t    }\n2107\t\n2108\t    /// Get the current router statistics.\n2109\t    pub fn router_stats(&self) -> forge_worker::router::RouterStats {\n2110\t        self.router.stats()\n2111\t    }\n2112\t\n2113\t    /// Get the recommended model tier for a task.\n2114\t    pub fn get_recommended_tier(&self, title: &str, labels: &[String]) -> forge_core::types::WorkerTier {\n2115\t        let complexity = self.score_task_complexity(title, None, labels);\n2116\t        match complexity {\n2117\t            0..=30 => forge_core::types::WorkerTier::Budget,\n2118\t            31..=60 => forge_core::types::WorkerTier::Standard,\n2119\t            _ => forge_core::types::WorkerTier::Premium,\n2120\t        }\n2121\t    }\n2122\t\n2123\t    /// Poll routing data for display updates.\n2124\t    ///\n2125\t    /// This should be called periodically to update routing stats\n2126\t    /// from the router's internal state.\n2127\t    pub fn poll_routing_data(&mut self) {\n2128\t        let should_poll = self.last_routing_poll.map_or(true, |t| {\n2129\t            t.elapsed().as_secs() >= 5 // Poll every 5 seconds\n2130\t        });\n2131\t\n2132\t        if !should_poll {\n2133\t            return;\n2134\t        }\n2135\t\n2136\t        // Sync router stats with routing data\n2137\t        let stats = self.router.stats();\n2138\t        self.routing_data.total_decisions = stats.total_decisions;\n2139\t        self.routing_data.by_tier = stats.by_tier.clone();\n2140\t        self.routing_data.by_model = stats.by_model.clone();\n2141\t        self.routing_data.by_reason = stats.by_reason.clone();\n2142\t\n2143\t        // Keep recent decisions from router history\n2144\t        let history = self.router.history();\n2145\t        self.routing_data.recent_decisions = history.iter().rev().take(20).cloned().collect();\n2146\t\n2147\t        // Calculate savings\n2148\t        self.routing_data.calculate_savings();\n2149\t        self.routing_data.last_update = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n2150\t        self.routing_data.is_loading = false;\n2151\t\n2152\t        self.last_routing_poll = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n2153\t    }\n2154\t\n2155\t    /// Update from server state (server-client synchronization).\n2156\t    ///\n2157\t    /// This method is called when the TUI is connected to a FORGE server\n2158\t    /// and receives state updates. The server provides authoritative\n2159\t    /// worker and bead state that should be reflected in the UI.\n2160\t    pub fn update_from_server_state(\n2161\t        &mut self,\n2162\t        workers: Vec<forge_server::protocol::WorkerState>,\n2163\t        beads: Vec<forge_server::protocol::BeadState>,\n2164\t    ) {\n2165\t        use crate::status::WorkerStatusFile;\n2166\t\n2167\t        // Data management for the FORGE TUI.\n//!\n//! This module provides the data layer for the TUI, managing worker state,\n//! task information, cost analytics, and formatting data for display. It integrates with\n//! forge-core's StatusWatcher to provide real-time updates, forge-worker's\n//! tmux discovery for additional session information, the BeadManager\n//! for task queue data from monitored workspaces, forge-cost for cost analytics,\n//! LogWatcher for real-time log parsing, HealthMonitor for worker health tracking,\n//! and AlertManager for worker health alerts.\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\n\nuse crate::activity_panel::{ActivityEntry, ActivityEventType, ActivityLogData};\nuse crate::alert::{AlertBadge, AlertManager, AlertNotifier, AlertSeverity, AlertType};\nuse crate::bead::BeadManager;\nuse crate::cost_panel::{BudgetConfig, CostPanelData};\nuse crate::log_watcher::{LogWatcher, LogWatcherConfig, LogWatcherEvent, RealtimeMetrics};\nuse crate::metrics_panel::MetricsPanelData;\nuse crate::perf_metrics::{get_memory_rss, PerfMetrics};\nuse crate::routing_panel::RoutingData;\nuse crate::status::{StatusWatcher, StatusWatcherConfig, WorkerCounts, WorkerStatusFile};\nuse crate::subscription_panel::SubscriptionData;\nuse forge_core::activity_monitor::{ActivityMonitor, ActivityState, WorkerActivity};\nuse forge_core::types::WorkerStatus;\nuse forge_cost::{CostDatabase, CostQuery, SubscriptionTracker};\nuse forge_worker::complexity::{ComplexityScorer, TaskContext};\nuse forge_worker::discovery::DiscoveryResult;\nuse forge_worker::health::{\n    HealthCheckType, HealthLevel, HealthMonitor, HealthMonitorConfig, WorkerHealthStatus,\n};\nuse forge_worker::router::{Router, TaskMetadata};\nuse forge_core::types::Priority;\n\n/// Aggregated worker data for TUI display.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct WorkerData {\n    /// All known workers by ID (from status files)\n    pub workers: HashMap<String, WorkerStatusFile>,\n    /// Counts by status\n    pub counts: WorkerCounts,\n    /// Last update timestamp\n    pub last_update: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Discovered tmux sessions (supplement to status files)\n    pub tmux_sessions: Option<DiscoveryResult>,\n    /// Last tmux discovery timestamp\n    pub tmux_last_update: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Health status per worker\n    pub health_status: HashMap<String, WorkerHealthStatus>,\n    /// Last health check timestamp\n    pub health_last_update: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Activity status per worker (idle/working/stuck)\n    pub activity_status: HashMap<String, WorkerActivity>,\n    /// Last activity check timestamp\n    pub activity_last_update: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n}\n\nimpl WorkerData {\n    /// Create empty worker data (loading state).\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Update from a StatusWatcher.\n    pub fn update_from_watcher(&mut self, watcher: &StatusWatcher) {\n        self.workers = watcher.workers().clone();\n        self.counts = watcher.worker_counts();\n        self.last_update = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n    }\n\n    /// Update from tmux discovery result.\n    pub fn update_from_tmux(&mut self, result: DiscoveryResult) {\n        self.tmux_sessions = Some(result);\n        self.tmux_last_update = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n    }\n\n    /// Update health status from health monitor.\n    pub fn update_health_status(&mut self, health: HashMap<String, WorkerHealthStatus>) {\n        self.health_status = health;\n        self.health_last_update = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n    }\n\n    /// Get health status for a specific worker.\n    pub fn get_health(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Option<&WorkerHealthStatus> {\n        self.health_status.get(worker_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Count workers by health level.\n    pub fn health_counts(&self) -> (usize, usize, usize) {\n        let mut healthy = 0;\n        let mut degraded = 0;\n        let mut unhealthy = 0;\n\n        for health in self.health_status.values() {\n            match health.health_level() {\n                HealthLevel::Healthy => healthy += 1,\n                HealthLevel::Degraded => degraded += 1,\n                HealthLevel::Unhealthy => unhealthy += 1,\n            }\n        }\n\n        (healthy, degraded, unhealthy)\n    }\n\n    /// Update activity status from activity monitor.\n    pub fn update_activity_status(&mut self, activity: HashMap<String, WorkerActivity>) {\n        self.activity_status = activity;\n        self.activity_last_update = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n    }\n\n    /// Update from server worker states (team collaboration sync).\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the TUI is connected to a FORGE server\n    /// and receives authoritative worker state updates.\n    pub fn update_from_server(&mut self, server_workers: HashMap<String, crate::status::WorkerStatusFile>) {\n        // Replace workers with server state (server is authoritative)\n        self.workers = server_workers;\n\n        // Recalculate counts\n        let mut counts = WorkerCounts::default();\n        for worker in self.workers.values() {\n            match worker.status {\n                WorkerStatus::Active => counts.active += 1,\n                WorkerStatus::Idle => counts.idle += 1,\n                WorkerStatus::Starting => counts.starting += 1,\n                WorkerStatus::Failed => counts.failed += 1,\n                WorkerStatus::Stopped => counts.stopped += 1,\n                WorkerStatus::Error => counts.error += 1,\n                WorkerStatus::Paused => counts.paused += 1,\n            }\n            counts.total += 1;\n        }\n        self.counts = counts;\n        self.last_update = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n    }\n\n    /// Get activity status for a specific worker.\n    pub fn get_activity(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Option<&WorkerActivity> {\n        self.activity_status.get(worker_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get workers that are currently stuck (have task but no activity > 15 min).\n    pub fn stuck_workers(&self) -> Vec<&WorkerActivity> {\n        self.activity_status\n            .values()\n            .filter(|a| a.state == ActivityState::Stuck)\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Count workers by activity state (idle, working, stuck).\n    pub fn activity_counts(&self) -> (usize, usize, usize) {\n        let mut idle = 0;\n        let mut working = 0;\n        let mut stuck = 0;\n\n        for activity in self.activity_status.values() {\n            match activity.state {\n                ActivityState::Idle => idle += 1,\n                ActivityState::Working => working += 1,\n                ActivityState::Stuck | ActivityState::Unresponsive => stuck += 1,\n                ActivityState::Unknown => {}\n            }\n        }\n\n        (idle, working, stuck)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if data has been loaded.\n    pub fn is_loaded(&self) -> bool {\n        self.last_update.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there are no workers (from status files).\n    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n        self.workers.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if there are any workers from either source.\n    pub fn has_any_workers(&self) -> bool {\n        !self.workers.is_empty()\n            || self\n                .tmux_sessions\n                .as_ref()\n                .map_or(false, |s| !s.workers.is_empty())\n    }\n\n    /// Get total worker count from all sources.\n    pub fn total_worker_count(&self) -> usize {\n        // Prefer status file count, fall back to tmux\n        if !self.workers.is_empty() {\n            self.workers.len()\n        } else {\n            self.tmux_sessions.as_ref().map_or(0, |s| s.workers.len())\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get workers grouped by model.\n    pub fn workers_by_model(&self) -> HashMap<String, Vec<&WorkerStatusFile>> {\n        let mut by_model: HashMap<String, Vec<&WorkerStatusFile>> = HashMap::new();\n        for worker in self.workers.values() {\n            let model = if worker.model.is_empty() {\n                \"unknown\".to_string()\n            } else {\n                worker.model.clone()\n            };\n            by_model.entry(model).or_default().push(worker);\n        }\n        by_model\n    }\n\n    /// Format worker pool summary for display.\n    pub fn format_worker_pool_summary(&self) -> String {\n        self.format_worker_pool_summary_with_paused(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Format worker pool summary for display with paused worker count.\n    pub fn format_worker_pool_summary_with_paused(&self, paused_count: usize) -> String {\n        if !self.is_loaded() {\n            return \"Loading worker data...\".to_string();\n        }\n\n        // Check both status files and tmux sessions\n        if !self.has_any_workers() {\n            return \"No workers found.\\n\\n\\\n                    Start workers using:\\n\\\n                    - ./scripts/spawn-workers.sh\\n\\\n                    - Or create status files in ~/.forge/status/\"\n                .to_string();\n        }\n\n        let mut lines = Vec::new();\n\n        // If we have status file data, use it (more detailed)\n        if !self.is_empty() {\n            let c = &self.counts;\n            // Include paused count in total line if any workers are paused\n            let total_line = if paused_count > 0 {\n                format!(\n                    \"Total: {} ({} active, {} idle, ⏸{} paused)\",\n                    c.total, c.active, c.idle, paused_count\n                )\n            } else {\n                format!(\n                    \"Total: {} ({} active, {} idle)\",\n                    c.total, c.active, c.idle\n                )\n            };\n            lines.push(total_line);\n\n            // Show health summary\n            if !self.health_status.is_empty() {\n                let (healthy, degraded, unhealthy) = self.health_counts();\n                if unhealthy > 0 {\n                    lines.push(format!(\n                        \"Health: {} ● | {} ◐ | {} ○\",\n                        healthy, degraded, unhealthy\n                    ));\n                } else if degraded > 0 {\n                    lines.push(format!(\"Health: {} ● | {} ◐\", healthy, degraded));\n                } else {\n                    lines.push(format!(\"Health: {} ● (all healthy)\", healthy));\n                }\n            }\n\n            if c.unhealthy() > 0 {\n                lines.push(format!(\"Unhealthy: {}\", c.unhealthy()));\n            }\n            lines.push(String::new());\n\n            // Workers by model\n            let by_model = self.workers_by_model();\n            let mut models: Vec<_> = by_model.keys().cloned().collect();\n            models.sort();\n\n            for model in models {\n                if let Some(workers) = by_model.get(&model) {\n                    let active = workers\n                        .iter()\n                        .filter(|w| w.status == WorkerStatus::Active)\n                        .count();\n                    let idle = workers\n                        .iter()\n                        .filter(|w| w.status == WorkerStatus::Idle)\n                        .count();\n\n                    let display_name = format_model_name(&model);\n                    lines.push(format!(\n                        \"{:<10} {} active, {} idle\",\n                        display_name, active, idle\n                    ));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If we have tmux sessions, show supplemental info\n        if let Some(ref tmux) = self.tmux_sessions {\n            if !tmux.workers.is_empty() {\n                if !self.is_empty() {\n                    lines.push(String::new());\n                    lines.push(\"Tmux Sessions:\".to_string());\n                } else {\n                    // Tmux-only data (no status files)\n                    lines.push(format!(\n                        \"Total: {} ({} attached, {} detached)\",\n                        tmux.workers.len(),\n                        tmux.attached_count,\n                        tmux.detached_count\n                    ));\n                    lines.push(String::new());\n                }\n\n                // Show by type\n                let mut types: Vec<_> = tmux.by_type.iter().collect();\n                types.sort_by_key(|(t, _)| t.short_name());\n\n                for (worker_type, count) in types {\n                    lines.push(format!(\"{:<10} {}\", worker_type.short_name(), count));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        lines.join(\"\\n\")\n    }\n\n    /// Format worker table for Workers view.\n    pub fn format_worker_table(&self) -> String {\n        if !self.is_loaded() {\n            return \"Loading worker data...\".to_string();\n        }\n\n        // Check both sources\n        if !self.has_any_workers() {\n            return \"No workers found.\\n\\n\\\n                    Workers will appear here when they register\\n\\\n                    status files in ~/.forge/status/\\n\\n\\\n                    [G] Spawn GLM  [S] Spawn Sonnet  [O] Spawn Opus  [K] Kill\"\n                .to_string();\n        }\n\n        let mut lines = Vec::new();\n\n        // If we have status file workers, show the detailed table with health\n        if !self.is_empty() {\n            // Show health summary if we have health data\n            if !self.health_status.is_empty() {\n                let (healthy, degraded, unhealthy) = self.health_counts();\n                let total = healthy + degraded + unhealthy;\n                lines.push(format!(\n                    \"Health: {} healthy | {} degraded | {} unhealthy | {} total\",\n                    healthy, degraded, unhealthy, total\n                ));\n                lines.push(String::new());\n            }\n\n            lines.push(\"┌───┬─────────────────┬──────────┬──────────┬─────────────┐\".to_string());\n            lines.push(\"│ H │ Worker ID       │ Model    │ Status   │ Task        │\".to_string());\n            lines.push(\"├───┼─────────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────────┤\".to_string());\n\n            let mut workers: Vec<_> = self.workers.values().collect();\n            workers.sort_by(|a, b| a.worker_id.cmp(&b.worker_id));\n\n            for worker in workers.iter().take(10) {\n                let health_indicator = self\n                    .get_health(&worker.worker_id)\n                    .map(|h| h.health_indicator())\n                    .unwrap_or(\"?\");\n                let worker_id = truncate_string(&worker.worker_id, 15);\n                let model = format_model_name_short(&worker.model);\n                let status = format_status(&worker.status);\n                let task = worker\n                    .current_task\n                    .as_deref()\n                    .map(|t| truncate_string(t, 11))\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|| \"-\".to_string());\n\n                lines.push(format!(\n                    \"│ {} │ {:<15} │ {:<8} │ {:<8} │ {:<11} │\",\n                    health_indicator, worker_id, model, status, task\n                ));\n            }\n\n            lines.push(\"└───┴─────────────────┴──────────┴──────────┴─────────────┘\".to_string());\n\n            if self.workers.len() > 10 {\n                lines.push(format!(\n                    \"\\n... and {} more workers\",\n                    self.workers.len() - 10\n                ));\n            }\n        } else if let Some(ref tmux) = self.tmux_sessions {\n            // Fall back to tmux session table\n            lines.push(\"┌────────────────────────┬──────────┬──────────┬──────────┐\".to_string());\n            lines.push(\"│ Session Name           │ Model    │ Status   │ Age      │\".to_string());\n            lines.push(\"├────────────────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┤\".to_string());\n\n            let mut workers: Vec<_> = tmux.workers.iter().collect();\n            workers.sort_by(|a, b| a.session_name.cmp(&b.session_name));\n\n            for worker in workers.iter().take(10) {\n                let session = truncate_string(&worker.session_name, 22);\n                let model = worker.worker_type.short_name();\n                let status = if worker.is_attached {\n                    \"attached\"\n                } else {\n                    \"detached\"\n                };\n                let age = worker.age();\n\n                lines.push(format!(\n                    \"│ {:<22} │ {:<8} │ {:<8} │ {:<8} │\",\n                    session, model, status, age\n                ));\n            }\n\n            lines.push(\"└────────────────────────┴──────────┴──────────┴──────────┘\".to_string());\n\n            if tmux.workers.len() > 10 {\n                lines.push(format!(\n                    \"\\n... and {} more sessions\",\n                    tmux.workers.len() - 10\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n\n        lines.push(\"\\n[G] Spawn GLM  [S] Spawn Sonnet  [O] Spawn Opus  [K] Kill\".to_string());\n\n        lines.join(\"\\n\")\n    }\n\n    /// Format activity log from worker data.\n    pub fn format_activity_log(&self) -> String {\n        if !self.is_loaded() {\n            return \"Loading activity data...\".to_string();\n        }\n\n        if !self.has_any_workers() {\n            return \"No recent activity.\\n\\n\\\n                    Activity will appear here as workers\\n\\\n                    complete tasks and update their status.\"\n                .to_string();\n        }\n\n        let mut lines = Vec::new();\n\n        // Use status file workers if available (more detailed)\n        if !self.is_empty() {\n            let mut workers: Vec<_> = self.workers.values().collect();\n            workers.sort_by(|a, b| {\n                b.last_activity\n                    .cmp(&a.last_activity)\n                    .then(b.started_at.cmp(&a.started_at))\n            });\n\n            let now = chrono::Local::now();\n\n            for worker in workers.iter().take(10) {\n                let time_str = if let Some(activity) = worker.last_activity {\n                    activity.format(\"%H:%M:%S\").to_string()\n                } else if let Some(started) = worker.started_at {\n                    started.format(\"%H:%M:%S\").to_string()\n                } else {\n                    now.format(\"%H:%M:%S\").to_string()\n                };\n\n                let icon = match worker.status {\n                    WorkerStatus::Active => \"⟳\",\n                    WorkerStatus::Idle => \"💤\",\n                    WorkerStatus::Starting => \"🔄\",\n                    WorkerStatus::Failed => \"❌\",\n                    WorkerStatus::Stopped => \"⏹\",\n                    WorkerStatus::Error => \"⚠\",\n                    WorkerStatus::Paused => \"⏸\",\n                };\n\n                let message = match worker.status {\n                    WorkerStatus::Active => {\n                        if let Some(task) = &worker.current_task {\n                            format!(\"{} working on {}\", worker.worker_id, task)\n                        } else {\n                            format!(\"{} is active\", worker.worker_id)\n                        }\n                    }\n                    WorkerStatus::Idle => format!(\"{} idle\", worker.worker_id),\n                    WorkerStatus::Starting => format!(\"Spawned {}\", worker.worker_id),\n                    WorkerStatus::Failed => format!(\"{} failed\", worker.worker_id),\n                    WorkerStatus::Stopped => format!(\"{} stopped\", worker.worker_id),\n                    WorkerStatus::Error => format!(\"{} error\", worker.worker_id),\n                    WorkerStatus::Paused => format!(\"{} paused\", worker.worker_id),\n                };\n\n                lines.push(format!(\"{} {} {}\", time_str, icon, message));\n            }\n        } else if let Some(ref tmux) = self.tmux_sessions {\n            // Fall back to tmux session activity\n            let mut workers: Vec<_> = tmux.workers.iter().collect();\n            workers.sort_by_key(|b| std::cmp::Reverse(b.last_activity));\n\n            for worker in workers.iter().take(10) {\n                let time_str = worker.last_activity.format(\"%H:%M:%S\").to_string();\n                let icon = if worker.is_attached { \"⟳\" } else { \"💤\" };\n                let status = if worker.is_attached {\n                    \"attached\"\n                } else {\n                    \"detached\"\n                };\n                let message = format!(\"{} {} ({})\", worker.session_name, status, worker.age());\n\n                lines.push(format!(\"{} {} {}\", time_str, icon, message));\n            }\n        }\n\n        if lines.is_empty() {\n            lines.push(\"No recent activity.\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        lines.join(\"\\n\")\n    }\n}\n\n/// Format a model identifier for display.\nfn format_model_name(model: &str) -> String {\n    // Common model name mappings\n    let lower = model.to_lowercase();\n    if lower.contains(\"glm-47\") || lower.contains(\"glm47\") || lower.contains(\"glm-4.7\") {\n        \"GLM-4.7\".to_string()\n    } else if lower.contains(\"sonnet\") {\n        \"Sonnet\".to_string()\n    } else if lower.contains(\"opus\") {\n        \"Opus\".to_string()\n    } else if lower.contains(\"haiku\") {\n        \"Haiku\".to_string()\n    } else if model.is_empty() {\n        \"Unknown\".to_string()\n    } else {\n        model.to_string()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Format model name for table (shorter version).\nfn format_model_name_short(model: &str) -> String {\n    let name = format_model_name(model);\n    truncate_string(&name, 8)\n}\n\n/// Format worker status for display.\nfn format_status(status: &WorkerStatus) -> String {\n    match status {\n        WorkerStatus::Active => \"Active\".to_string(),\n        WorkerStatus::Idle => \"Idle\".to_string(),\n        WorkerStatus::Starting => \"Starting\".to_string(),\n        WorkerStatus::Failed => \"Failed\".to_string(),\n        WorkerStatus::Stopped => \"Stopped\".to_string(),\n        WorkerStatus::Error => \"Error\".to_string(),\n        WorkerStatus::Paused => \"Paused\".to_string(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Truncate a string to a maximum length.\nfn truncate_string(s: &str, max_len: usize) -> String {\n    if s.len() <= max_len {\n        s.to_string()\n    } else if max_len > 2 {\n        format!(\"{}…\", &s[..max_len - 1])\n    } else {\n        s[..max_len].to_string()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Interval for tmux discovery polling (5 seconds).\nconst TMUX_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL_SECS: u64 = 5;\n\n/// Interval for cost data polling (10 seconds).\nconst COST_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS: u64 = 10;\n\n/// Interval for metrics data polling (10 seconds).\nconst METRICS_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS: u64 = 10;\n\n/// Interval for log watcher polling (500ms).\nconst LOG_WATCHER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS: u64 = 500;\n\n/// Interval for subscription data polling (60 seconds).\nconst SUBSCRIPTION_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS: u64 = 60;\n\n/// Interval for health monitoring (30 seconds).\nconst HEALTH_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS: u64 = 30;\n\n/// Interval for activity monitoring (15 seconds).\nconst ACTIVITY_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS: u64 = 15;\n\n/// Data manager that handles the StatusWatcher, BeadManager, CostDatabase, LogWatcher, HealthMonitor, AlertManager, ActivityMonitor, and provides formatted data.\npub struct DataManager {\n    /// StatusWatcher for real-time updates\n    watcher: Option<StatusWatcher>,\n    /// Cached worker data\n    pub worker_data: WorkerData,\n    /// Bead manager for task queue data\n    pub bead_manager: BeadManager,\n    /// Cost database for analytics\n    cost_db: Option<CostDatabase>,\n    /// Cached cost panel data\n    pub cost_data: CostPanelData,\n    /// Subscription tracker for subscription management\n    subscription_tracker: SubscriptionTracker,\n    /// Subscription tracking data\n    pub subscription_data: SubscriptionData,\n    /// Performance metrics data\n    pub metrics_data: MetricsPanelData,\n    /// Real-time metrics from log parsing\n    pub realtime_metrics: RealtimeMetrics,\n    /// Health monitor for worker health checks\n    health_monitor: Option<HealthMonitor>,\n    /// Activity monitor for idle/stuck detection\n    activity_monitor: ActivityMonitor,\n    /// Alert manager for worker health alerts\n    pub alert_manager: AlertManager,\n    /// Alert notifier for terminal bell and visual notifications\n    pub alert_notifier: AlertNotifier,\n    /// Activity log data for real-time streaming display\n    pub activity_data: ActivityLogData,\n    /// FORGE internal performance metrics (event loop, render, memory)\n    pub perf_metrics: PerfMetrics,\n    /// Error message if watcher failed to initialize\n    init_error: Option<String>,\n    /// Router for intelligent model assignment\n    router: Router,\n    /// Complexity scorer for task analysis\n    complexity_scorer: ComplexityScorer,\n    /// Routing analytics data for display\n    pub routing_data: RoutingData,\n    /// Last routing poll time\n    last_routing_poll: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Last tmux discovery time\n    last_tmux_poll: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Last cost poll time\n    last_cost_poll: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Last metrics poll time\n    last_metrics_poll: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Last log watcher poll time\n    last_log_poll: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Last subscription poll time\n    last_subscription_poll: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Last health check time\n    last_health_poll: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Last activity monitor poll time\n    last_activity_poll: Option<std::time::Instant>,\n    /// Tokio runtime for async tmux discovery\n    runtime: Option<tokio::runtime::Runtime>,\n    /// Dirty flag - whether data changed since last check\n    dirty: bool,\n    /// Cached worker count for quick comparison\n    cached_worker_count: usize,\n    /// Cached bead count for quick comparison\n    cached_bead_count: usize,\n    /// Log watcher for real-time API usage parsing\n    log_watcher: Option<LogWatcher>,\n    /// Log watcher event receiver\n    log_rx: Option<std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<LogWatcherEvent>>,\n    /// Previous worker statuses for change detection\n    prev_worker_statuses: HashMap<String, WorkerStatus>,\n    /// Server bead state for team collaboration sync\n    server_beads: Vec<forge_server::protocol::BeadState>,\n}\n\nimpl DataManager {\n    /// Check if data changed since last poll.\n    pub fn is_dirty(&self) -> bool {\n        self.dirty\n    }\n\n    /// Clear the dirty flag.\n    pub fn clear_dirty(&mut self) {\n        self.dirty = false;\n    }\n\n    /// Take and clear the dirty flag.\n    pub fn take_dirty(&mut self) -> bool {\n        if self.dirty {\n            self.dirty = false;\n            true\n        } else {\n            false\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new DataManager, initializing the StatusWatcher, BeadManager, and CostDatabase.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        use std::time::Instant;\n        use tracing::info;\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ DataManager::new() started\");\n\n        // In test mode, skip creating the StatusWatcher to avoid exhausting inotify limits\n        // Tests that need file watching should create their own watchers with isolated directories\n        let (watcher, init_error) = if cfg!(test) {\n            (None, None)\n        } else {\n            match StatusWatcher::new(StatusWatcherConfig::default()) {\n                Ok(w) => (Some(w), None),\n                Err(e) => (\n                    None,\n                    Some(format!(\"Failed to initialize status watcher: {}\", e)),\n                ),\n            }\n        };\n        info!(\"⏱️ StatusWatcher initialized in {:?}\", start.elapsed());\n\n        // Create a tokio runtime for async tmux discovery\n        let runtime_start = Instant::now();\n        let runtime = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .ok();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Tokio runtime created in {:?}\", runtime_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Initialize bead manager with default workspaces\n        let bead_start = Instant::now();\n        let mut bead_manager = BeadManager::new();\n        bead_manager.add_default_workspaces();\n        info!(\"⏱️ BeadManager initialized in {:?}\", bead_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Initialize cost database from default location\n        let cost_start = Instant::now();\n        let cost_db = Self::init_cost_database();\n        let cost_data = CostPanelData::loading();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Cost database initialized in {:?}\", cost_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Initialize metrics data\n        let metrics_data = MetricsPanelData::loading();\n\n        // Initialize subscription tracker and data\n        let sub_start = Instant::now();\n        let subscription_tracker = SubscriptionTracker::with_default_config();\n        let subscription_data = Self::build_subscription_data(&subscription_tracker);\n        info!(\n            \"⏱️ Subscription tracker initialized in {:?}\",\n            sub_start.elapsed()\n        );\n\n        // Initialize real-time metrics from log parsing\n        let realtime_metrics = RealtimeMetrics::new();\n\n        // Initialize log watcher for real-time API usage parsing\n        let (log_watcher, log_rx) = match LogWatcher::new(LogWatcherConfig::default()) {\n            Ok((watcher, rx)) => (Some(watcher), Some(rx)),\n            Err(e) => {\n                info!(\"Failed to initialize log watcher: {}\", e);\n                (None, None)\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Initialize health monitor\n        let health_start = Instant::now();\n        let health_monitor = match HealthMonitor::new(HealthMonitorConfig::default()) {\n            Ok(m) => {\n                info!(\n                    \"⏱️ HealthMonitor initialized in {:?}\",\n                    health_start.elapsed()\n                );\n                Some(m)\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                info!(\"Failed to initialize health monitor: {}\", e);\n                None\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Initialize alert manager for worker health alerts\n        let alert_manager = AlertManager::new(100);\n\n        // Initialize alert notifier for terminal bell and visual notifications\n        let alert_notifier = AlertNotifier::new();\n\n        // Initialize activity monitor for idle/stuck detection\n        let activity_monitor = ActivityMonitor::with_defaults();\n\n        // Initialize activity log data\n        let activity_data = ActivityLogData::with_default_capacity();\n\n        // Initialize FORGE internal performance metrics\n        let perf_metrics = PerfMetrics::new();\n\n        let manager = Self {\n            watcher,\n            worker_data: WorkerData::new(),\n            bead_manager,\n            cost_db,\n            cost_data,\n            subscription_tracker,\n            subscription_data,\n            metrics_data,\n            realtime_metrics,\n            health_monitor,\n            activity_monitor,\n            alert_manager,\n            alert_notifier,\n            activity_data,\n            perf_metrics,\n            init_error,\n            router: Router::new(),\n            complexity_scorer: ComplexityScorer::new(),\n            routing_data: RoutingData::new(),\n            last_tmux_poll: None,\n            last_cost_poll: None,\n            last_metrics_poll: None,\n            last_log_poll: None,\n            last_subscription_poll: None,\n            last_health_poll: None,\n            last_activity_poll: None,\n            last_routing_poll: None,\n            runtime,\n            dirty: false,\n            cached_worker_count: 0,\n            cached_bead_count: 0,\n            log_watcher,\n            log_rx,\n            prev_worker_statuses: HashMap::new(),\n            server_beads: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        // Skip initial poll_updates during initialization - it blocks for too long\n        // due to bead manager calling `br` commands which can take 20+ seconds each.\n        // Let the first poll happen during the main loop instead.\n        info!(\"⏱️ Skipping initial poll_updates (will poll in main loop)\");\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ DataManager::new() completed in {:?}\", start.elapsed());\n        manager\n    }\n\n    /// Initialize the cost database.\n    fn init_cost_database() -> Option<CostDatabase> {\n        // Try default location: ~/.forge/costs.db\n        let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").ok()?;\n        let db_path = PathBuf::from(home).join(\".forge\").join(\"costs.db\");\n\n        if db_path.exists() {\n            CostDatabase::open(&db_path).ok()\n        } else {\n            // Try to create it\n            if let Some(parent) = db_path.parent() {\n                std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).ok()?;\n            }\n            CostDatabase::open(&db_path).ok()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a DataManager with a custom status directory.\n    pub fn with_status_dir(status_dir: std::path::PathBuf) -> Self {\n        let config = StatusWatcherConfig::default().with_status_dir(status_dir);\n        let (watcher, init_error) = match StatusWatcher::new(config) {\n            Ok(w) => (Some(w), None),\n            Err(e) => (\n                None,\n                Some(format!(\"Failed to initialize status watcher: {}\", e)),\n            ),\n        };\n\n        let runtime = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .ok();\n\n        // Initialize bead manager with default workspaces\n        let mut bead_manager = BeadManager::new();\n        bead_manager.add_default_workspaces();\n\n        // Initialize cost database\n        let cost_db = Self::init_cost_database();\n        let cost_data = CostPanelData::loading();\n\n        // Initialize metrics data\n        let metrics_data = MetricsPanelData::loading();\n\n        // Initialize subscription tracker and data\n        let subscription_tracker = SubscriptionTracker::with_default_config();\n        let subscription_data = Self::build_subscription_data(&subscription_tracker);\n\n        // Initialize real-time metrics from log parsing\n        let realtime_metrics = RealtimeMetrics::new();\n\n        // Initialize log watcher for real-time API usage parsing\n        let (log_watcher, log_rx) = match LogWatcher::new(LogWatcherConfig::default()) {\n            Ok((watcher, rx)) => (Some(watcher), Some(rx)),\n            Err(_) => (None, None),\n        };\n\n        // Initialize health monitor\n        let health_monitor = HealthMonitor::new(HealthMonitorConfig::default()).ok();\n\n        // Initialize alert manager for worker health alerts\n        let alert_manager = AlertManager::new(100);\n\n        // Initialize alert notifier for terminal bell and visual notifications\n        let alert_notifier = AlertNotifier::new();\n\n        // Initialize activity monitor for idle/stuck detection\n        let activity_monitor = ActivityMonitor::with_defaults();\n\n        // Initialize activity log data\n        let activity_data = ActivityLogData::with_default_capacity();\n\n        // Initialize FORGE internal performance metrics\n        let perf_metrics = PerfMetrics::new();\n\n        // Skip initial poll_updates during initialization - it blocks for too long\n        // due to bead manager calling `br` commands which can take seconds each.\n        // Let the first poll happen during the main loop instead.\n        Self {\n            watcher,\n            worker_data: WorkerData::new(),\n            bead_manager,\n            cost_db,\n            cost_data,\n            subscription_tracker,\n            subscription_data,\n            metrics_data,\n            realtime_metrics,\n            health_monitor,\n            activity_monitor,\n            alert_manager,\n            alert_notifier,\n            activity_data,\n            perf_metrics,\n            init_error,\n            router: Router::new(),\n            complexity_scorer: ComplexityScorer::new(),\n            routing_data: RoutingData::new(),\n            last_tmux_poll: None,\n            last_cost_poll: None,\n            last_metrics_poll: None,\n            last_log_poll: None,\n            last_subscription_poll: None,\n            last_health_poll: None,\n            last_activity_poll: None,\n            last_routing_poll: None,\n            runtime,\n            dirty: false,\n            cached_worker_count: 0,\n            cached_bead_count: 0,\n            log_watcher,\n            log_rx,\n            prev_worker_statuses: HashMap::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Build SubscriptionData from the tracker.\n    fn build_subscription_data(tracker: &SubscriptionTracker) -> SubscriptionData {\n        use crate::subscription_panel::{\n            ResetPeriod, SubscriptionAction, SubscriptionService, SubscriptionStatus,\n        };\n        use chrono::{Duration, Utc};\n        use tracing::info;\n\n        info!(\n            \"Building subscription data, tracker has {} subscriptions\",\n            tracker.len()\n        );\n\n        let mut data = SubscriptionData::new();\n\n        for summary in tracker.get_summaries() {\n            info!(\n                \"Processing subscription: {} (usage: {}/{:?})\",\n                summary.name, summary.quota_used, summary.quota_limit\n            );\n            // Map subscription name to service type\n            let service = if summary.name.to_lowercase().contains(\"anthropic\")\n                || summary.name.to_lowercase().contains(\"claude\")\n            {\n                SubscriptionService::ClaudePro\n            } else if summary.name.to_lowercase().contains(\"openai\")\n                || summary.name.to_lowercase().contains(\"chatgpt\")\n            {\n                SubscriptionService::ChatGPTPlus\n            } else if summary.name.to_lowercase().contains(\"cursor\") {\n                SubscriptionService::CursorPro\n            } else {\n                SubscriptionService::DeepSeekAPI // Default fallback\n            };\n\n            let mut status = SubscriptionStatus::new(service);\n\n            // Set usage if we have a limit\n            if
